what_it_does: An HTML/CSS desktop design mockup of a full-detail "workshop" overlay for a single selected graph node (the ask process). It renders the node identity, address, full output, read-only config, ports, status, and provenance layer over a dimmed canvas, with extensive inline documentation about what is grounded in existing code versus proposed.
observations: Large HTML comment header labels this as a staging probe for GAP-LIST #1, not registered in register.json, written to _panel-staging.
Implements a near-full-screen workshop overlay (ui://workshop) on top of a blurred/scrimmed canvas with a faint "ghost" node behind it.
Layout uses CSS grid areas: top, rail, canvas, foot, panel.
Header shows kind chip, node title/id, render-mode tag, status dot, and close control.
Address strip presents both a type address (ui://canvas/node) and a live instance ref (run://codebase/ask), plus a provenance layer label (ORIGIN).
Main column displays a "Full output" block with pre-wrap sample text about the memo gate / scheduler.
Side column shows read-only config (model, system, base_url, retries) and ports (question/context in, answer out), with a note that editing belongs to inspector A3.
Many elements carry data-ui-ref attributes mapping ui:// handles (ui://workshop, ui://workshop/full-render, ui://workshop/config, etc.).
References external stylesheet ../design-system.css and uses CSS custom properties throughout.
Footer states live status, workshop open, render-mode workshop, openable.
standouts: Explicitly distinguishes the existing minimal workshop overlay (nodeType·nodeId, address, full output, close) from the proposed fuller surface.
Strong "no fiction" grounding notes: real config fields, real ports, real status values, no invented metrics/timing/cost.
Address split into static type handle (ui://) and live instance ref (run://).
Side column is deliberately read-only; editing is delegated to the inspector (A3).
conventions: Follows project naming conventions: P3 · A12, AREA A, JOURNEY-MOMENT, REPRESENTS[], GROUNDING NOTES, PROPOSED-vs-EXISTING.
Uses semantic CSS custom properties and BEM-ish class names.
Uses data-ui-ref attributes to annotate UI addressability.
Breaks convention by being an unregistered staging probe (not in register.json).
HTML comments contain code references and feature-inventory cross-references, acting as embedded documentation.
concerns: Heavy explanatory metadata in HTML comments may drift from actual code if not maintained.
The "Full output" sample is placeholder explanatory text about the memo gate rather than real ask output.
Hard-coded node instance and values; no dynamic templating visible.
Interactive controls like the close button are spans with cursor:pointer, lacking button semantics or keyboard handlers.
No ARIA roles/labels visible on the overlay or controls.
notes: A staging desktop mockup for the full-detail 'workshop' overlay on the ask process node, grounded in real backend fields and explicitly scoped as read-only context with editing deferred to the inspector.
questions: How is the overlay populated with real node data — templating, client JS, or server render?
What is the intended responsive or mobile equivalent for this workshop surface?
Are the data-ui-ref attributes consumed by a test resolver or runtime, and where is that mapping validated?
Is the proposed side-column (config + ports) approved or still under design review?
What is the exact role and location of ../design-system.css relative to this mockup?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is a staging design probe that renders the proposed workshop full-detail surface and clearly labels which parts are grounded in existing code versus proposed extensions.
apparent_themes: Grounded design with no fictional data.
Identity as type plus instance (ui:// vs run://).
intention_signals: It is trying to define a credible, fully-detailed 'workshop' view for a single node that stays faithful to the real node model, while proposing a richer layout than the current minimal overlay and keeping editing concerns out of the workshop.
novelty: Explicitly frames the design as a staging probe with a 'no fiction' grounding section, splits a node's address into static type handle and live instance ref, and proposes read-only config/ports context alongside the real minimal overlay.
contribution: A concrete visual and structural proposal for the workshop render-mode, including the three-spine layout (node identity, address, full output), the type/instance address split, the read-only config/ports side column, and a pattern of annotating every interactive region with a ui:// data-ui-ref handle.
summary_for_embedding: An HTML/CSS desktop mockup of the P3 A12 'workshop' full-detail node surface for the ask process, showing a proposed overlay with node identity, address, full output, read-only config, and ports over a dimmed canvas.
intention_for_embedding: To propose and document a grounded, full-detail workshop view for a single graph node while distinguishing existing frontend behavior from proposed extensions.
